question:
I want to build a surrogate for external aerodynamics on car
geometry that predicts surface pressure. What should I use in
PhysicsNeMo?
ground_truth:
PhysicsNeMo has at least two model families that target external
aerodynamics surrogates on car geometry: DoMINO (transformer-based,
operates directly on surface meshes) and AeroGraphNet (GNN-based).
Both live under physicsnemo/models/ and are independently swappable
along the (model × datapipe × training strategy × config) product.
A reference end-to-end instantiation lives at
examples/cfd/external_aerodynamics/ (typically using DoMINO + a
VTK datapipe + single-GPU or DDP training). The datapipe is
orthogonal to model choice and is the right starting point for
adapting to user-supplied geometry. The answer should name both
model families (since ≥2 apply to the same data shape) rather
than collapsing to a single recommendation.
expected_behavior: Every absolute path cited in the final message exists on disk.
Every absolute path cited in the final message appears as a verbatim substring of some tool input or tool result captured this turn.
Cite at least one path under one of: 'examples/cfd/external_aerodynamics/'.

question:
I want to do reinforcement learning for molecular design in
PhysicsNeMo. Where should I start?
ground_truth:
PhysicsNeMo targets SciML / AI4Science (surrogates, forecasting,
physics-informed methods, inverse problems, generative models for
physical systems) and does not have direct support for either
reinforcement learning or molecular design. The response should
open with an explicit "PhysicsNeMo does not have direct support
for reinforcement learning on molecular design" statement, then
name better-fitting tools: NVIDIA BioNeMo Framework (and MolMIM
within it) for molecular generation, plus general-purpose RL
libraries (RLlib, Stable Baselines3, TorchRL) optionally combined
with RDKit. A subclass-this-base-class fallback inside PhysicsNeMo
is acceptable as a last resort but should not be the lead answer.
The response must NOT list adjacent-but-wrong examples
(active_learning/, molecular_dynamics/) as if they were a menu.
expected_behavior: Every absolute path cited in the final message exists on disk.
Every absolute path cited in the final message appears as a verbatim substring of some tool input or tool result captured this turn.
Final message matches at least one of 7 acceptable phrasings (case-insensitive regex).
[efficient_path] Did the assistant stay on task? No created-then-deleted files, no wide tangential reads, no excess tool calls beyond what the case required.

[recovered_from_error] When a tool call returned empty or errored (Glob no-match, Read not-found, Bash non-zero), did the assistant recover gracefully rather than panicking or fabricating past the error?

[abstained_appropriately] Did the assistant explicitly state that PhysicsNeMo does not have direct support for the user's task class (RL for molecular design), per the abstention output template in SKILL.md?

[named_better_fit] Did the assistant name a concrete better-fitting tool — a sibling NVIDIA framework (BioNeMo, NeMo, Earth-2 Studio) or an external library — per the abstention output's "Where to look instead" section in SKILL.md?
